summary: Diff implements username search/autocomplete (backend + reusable input), @-mention autocomplete in message/DM composers, quick profile popovers on avatars/names, and a new avatar upload component integrated into profile setup/settings and avatar rendering. Server/channel customization and role/permission presets are not present. Expanded profile fields and ID-visibility toggle are added in frontend UI/popover but lack corresponding backend persistence in the shown backend types/save logic.

missing_requirements.requirement: Add server and channel customization fields (server: name/description/icon/banner/settings; channel: name/topic/icon/settings) with backend persistence and frontend settings forms; lists/headers reflect updates.
missing_requirements.reason: No server/channel additional fields, settings UIs, or persistence logic are shown; backend Server/Channel types in the diff still only contain id/name/createdAt/messages/channels and no icon/banner/description/topic/settings fields.
evidence: frontend-file-summaries.txt (ServerList/ChannelList described as inline editing/edit-delete only; no settings UI mentioned)

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement server role/rank permission system with preset roles (Owner/Admin/Moderator/Member), enforcement for server/channel actions, and owner customization of permission flags with persistence.
missing_requirements.reason: No role data models, role assignment APIs, permission flag editing UI, or enforcement logic for roles are shown in the diff; only existing AccessControl permission checks for generic #user appear around profile/search.

missing_requirements.requirement: Expand user profiles with additional fields (bio/links/banner/status/badges/pronouns/theme color etc.) and update Profile/Settings page to edit/save; render new fields read-only in at least one context; show validation errors in English.
missing_requirements.reason: Frontend adds fields and renders them (settings page + popover), but backend UserProfile type and save/get methods shown do not include these fields, so saving/persisting them is not evidenced.
evidence: backend/main.mo (UserProfile only: displayName, avatarUrl, avatarAttachment; saveCallerUserProfile stores that type)
frontend/src/pages/ProfileSettingsPage.tsx (casts to ExtendedUserProfile and sends extra fields via `any`)

missing_requirements.requirement: Add a Profile/Settings toggle controlling visibility of the user principal/ID to others, and enforce it anywhere identity details are displayed (including popovers); owner can still see their own ID in settings.
missing_requirements.reason: Popover respects a `showIdToOthers` flag (and always shows for own profile), and settings UI includes a `showIdToOthers` state, but backend persistence for this flag is not shown (UserProfile type lacks it), so the toggle likely will not persist/propagate reliably.
